creator: tobias.rehbein@web.de
text:
(In reply to Alexey Dokuchaev from comment #28)

> Got it, thank you Tobias!  One last question: in the FixupTOC() function, when
> adjusting the end of the last audio track to be in the first session, Linux
> code first obtains `ms_str.addr.lba value' via ioctl(CDROMMULTISESSION) and
> then uses it in calculations if it's > 100, while FreeBSD code does not have
> this call/check and works with `dwStartSector' fields only.  

Not gonna lie, after all these years, I can no longer remember why I solved things that way back then. I think I have stolen this code from another CD rippers FreeBSD port, but i don't know which one. The `ioctl` was not working if I remember correctly.

> Also, breaking the the loop in Linux code would `return 1', while it's
> `return 0' for FreeBSD.  Is everything correct there, and do we need to add
> some explanatory comment for the future readers of this adjustment code?

I'm completely blank on this question, sorry. A few years ago I might have been able to answer, but I just don't remember (and fail to reconstruct the reasons) why I might have changed the return value. Unfortunately I neglected to write comments back then, too.
